target.name: Netrasemi
target.founder: Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, Sreejith Varma

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, and Sreejith Varma, having raised ₹107 Cr (~$13M) to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms.

Peer-median analysis is the applicable triangulation method. Four same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—yield a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on the $1.5B peer median. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which precludes public-comp validation and limits visibility into capital efficiency relative to peers. The wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M to $5.0B) reflects heterogeneity in technology focus and commercial traction within frontier semiconductor, introducing material range uncertainty. Stage-appropriate comparability is constrained by limited Series A precedent in the sector; early-stage semiconductor funding remains concentrated among well-capitalized founders and strategic backers.
